CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, val INTEGER, arg INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE additional_table (id INTEGER, val2 INTEGER);
SELECT arg_min_null(t.arg, t.val) FROM test_table t INNER JOIN additional_table a ON t.id = a.id WHERE a.val2 IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE nested_table (group_id INTEGER, id INTEGER, val INTEGER);
SELECT arg_min_null(n.val, n.id) FROM nested_table n GROUP BY n.group_id;
SELECT arg_min_null(arg, val) FROM (SELECT id, val, arg FROM test_table WHERE val > 0) AS filtered_table;
SELECT arg_min_null(arg, val) FROM (SELECT t.arg, COALESCE(t.val, 0) AS val FROM test_table t) AS coalesced_table WHERE val < 10;
CREATE TABLE multi_table (group_id INTEGER, val INTEGER, id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO multi_table VALUES (1, 5, 1), (1, NULL, 2), (1, 10, 3), (2, 7, 4), (2, NULL, 5);
SELECT arg_min_null(m.id, m.val) FROM multi_table m GROUP BY m.group_id HAVING COUNT(m.val) > 1;
